revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:不動明王, en:Acala); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-25) == 「真言を唱えることが越三昧耶戒に当たる」という説、様々な仏教関係書らしきものを典拠に書かれていましたが、不動明王信仰と本来何も関係がない上、ポピュラーな解説書では見たことも聞いたこともない奇説でしたので、削除致しました。あまりにも長文ですので残しようがなく４分の３を削りました。一つここの文章をお書きになった方に伺いたいのですが、不動信仰を行っている現代日本の諸寺院のうちで真言を唱えるなといっている寺院があるようでしたら、是非名前を出して頂ければと思います。私も不動信仰がガキの頃からでずいぶん長い人間ですし所々の不動奉安寺院にも参拝しましたが寡聞にして聞いたことのない説です。 --[[利用者:Touryuuuan|松平俊介]]（[[利用者‐会話:Touryuuuan|会話]]） 2014年11月13日 (木) 14:21 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25) == {{WikiProject banner shell|class=B|1= {{WikiProject Buddhism|importance=Mid}} {{WikiProject Japan|myth=|importance=mid|religion=y}} {{WikiProject China|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject East Asia|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Religion|importance=Top}} }} == Untitled == It appears to be odd that Acala is designated as cognate with Vairocana, rather than Akshobya - especially as Akshobya also means "The Immovable One" - however, I am not skilled in Shingon. So this is more of a query. "Akshobya is sometimes merged with Acala (Jp. Fudo myo-o), whose name also means the 'Immovable One' in Sanskrit. However, Acalanatha is not a Buddha, but one of the Five Wisdom Kings of the Womb Realm in Vajrayana. Prior to the advent of Bhaisajyaguru (Yakushi), Akshobya was the subject of a minor popular cult in Japan as healing Buddha." - http://www.sortlifeout.com/HH_Dalai_Lama-Glasgow2004-SECC.htm Appears to agree with me - so I shall make the change. ([[User:20040302|20040302]]) == Proposed merger == It has been proposed that [[Fudo]] be incorporated into [[Acala]]. I found the oneway merge tag and changed it so it can be referenced back. I myself have only a minute knowledge of Buddhism, therefore, I would like somebody with a better insight to comment. -- [[User:Emana|Emana]] 07:22, 24 February 2007 (UTC) :It appears that the merge has been done, but currently it's not clear what the connection is between Fudo and Acala. Perhaps someone can explain, with sources: Are they the variations of the same god, or merely similar? --[[User:Chriswaterguy|Chriswaterguy]] [[User talk:Chriswaterguy|talk]] 20:55, 23 August 2007 (UTC) ::My understanding is that they're the same thing, and Fudo myo-o (lit. immovable wisdom king) is just a translation of the meaning into Chinese/Japanese, as opposed to the Sanskrit reading. [[User:Jpatokal|Jpatokal]] 06:00, 24 August 2007 (UTC) ==LGBT?== According to LGBT themes in mythology, this figure is somehow LGBT-related. Does anyone know how, even if it is disputed? If not, i've tagged it for removal[[User:Yobmod|Yob]][[User talk:Yobmod|Mod]] 13:51, 20 March 2009 (UTC) == Korean/Chinese == Same Chinese characters reads 'Bu-dong myung-wang' in Korean. We should add Chinese characters and also Korean reading since it is popular in both areas. --[[Special:Contributions/128.227.69.240|128.227.69.240]] ([[User talk:128.227.69.240|talk]]) 22:28, 5 April 2009 (UTC) == Nice Met photos == Some nice Met acalas [http://www.metmuseum.org/art/collection/search#!/search?q=acala&perPage=20&sortBy=Relevance&sortOrder=asc&offset=0&pageSize=0 here]. Will add one or two when they get moved to Commons. —[[User:LuisVilla|Luis]] ([[User talk:LuisVilla|talk]]) 05:07, 11 February 2017 (UTC) == Errors in the mantra == According to my link, the mantra is also written in IAST, but based on the traditional writing in Siddham script - https://archive.org/details/mahavairocanasutrachikyoyamamotoadityaprakashan_769_B/page/54/mode/1up [[Special:Contributions/~2026-10972-22|~2026-10972-22]] ([[User talk:~2026-10972-22|talk]]) 14:46, 18 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:不動明王, en:Acala); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 18:42, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-25) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 18:42, 25 February 2026 (UTC) 「真言を唱えることが越三昧耶戒に当たる」という説、様々な仏教関係書らしきものを典拠に書かれていましたが、不動明王信仰と本来何も関係がない上、ポピュラーな解説書では見たことも聞いたこともない奇説でしたので、削除致しました。あまりにも長文ですので残しようがなく４分の３を削りました。一つここの文章をお書きになった方に伺いたいのですが、不動信仰を行っている現代日本の諸寺院のうちで真言を唱えるなといっている寺院があるようでしたら、是非名前を出して頂ければと思います。私も不動信仰がガキの頃からでずいぶん長い人間ですし所々の不動奉安寺院にも参拝しましたが寡聞にして聞いたことのない説です。 --[[利用者:Touryuuuan|松平俊介]]（[[利用者‐会話:Touryuuuan|会話]]） 2014年11月13日 (木) 14:21 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 18:42, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=B|1= {{WikiProject Buddhism|importance=Mid}} {{WikiProject Japan|myth=|importance=mid|religion=y}} {{WikiProject China|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject East Asia|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Religion|importance=Top}} }} == Untitled == It appears to be odd that Acala is designated as cognate with Vairocana, rather than Akshobya - especially as Akshobya also means "The Immovable One" - however, I am not skilled in Shingon. So this is more of a query. "Akshobya is sometimes merged with Acala (Jp. Fudo myo-o), whose name also means the 'Immovable One' in Sanskrit. However, Acalanatha is not a Buddha, but one of the Five Wisdom Kings of the Womb Realm in Vajrayana. Prior to the advent of Bhaisajyaguru (Yakushi), Akshobya was the subject of a minor popular cult in Japan as healing Buddha." - http://www.sortlifeout.com/HH_Dalai_Lama-Glasgow2004-SECC.htm Appears to agree with me - so I shall make the change. ([[User:20040302|20040302]]) == Proposed merger == It has been proposed that [[Fudo]] be incorporated into [[Acala]]. I found the oneway merge tag and changed it so it can be referenced back. I myself have only a minute knowledge of Buddhism, therefore, I would like somebody with a better insight to comment. -- [[User:Emana|Emana]] 07:22, 24 February 2007 (UTC) :It appears that the merge has been done, but currently it's not clear what the connection is between Fudo and Acala. Perhaps someone can explain, with sources: Are they the variations of the same god, or merely similar? --[[User:Chriswaterguy|Chriswaterguy]] [[User talk:Chriswaterguy|talk]] 20:55, 23 August 2007 (UTC) ::My understanding is that they're the same thing, and Fudo myo-o (lit. immovable wisdom king) is just a translation of the meaning into Chinese/Japanese, as opposed to the Sanskrit reading. [[User:Jpatokal|Jpatokal]] 06:00, 24 August 2007 (UTC) ==LGBT?== According to LGBT themes in mythology, this figure is somehow LGBT-related. Does anyone know how, even if it is disputed? If not, i've tagged it for removal[[User:Yobmod|Yob]][[User talk:Yobmod|Mod]] 13:51, 20 March 2009 (UTC) == Korean/Chinese == Same Chinese characters reads 'Bu-dong myung-wang' in Korean. We should add Chinese characters and also Korean reading since it is popular in both areas. --[[Special:Contributions/128.227.69.240|128.227.69.240]] ([[User talk:128.227.69.240|talk]]) 22:28, 5 April 2009 (UTC) == Nice Met photos == Some nice Met acalas [http://www.metmuseum.org/art/collection/search#!/search?q=acala&perPage=20&sortBy=Relevance&sortOrder=asc&offset=0&pageSize=0 here]. Will add one or two when they get moved to Commons. —[[User:LuisVilla|Luis]] ([[User talk:LuisVilla|talk]]) 05:07, 11 February 2017 (UTC) == Errors in the mantra == According to my link, the mantra is also written in IAST, but based on the traditional writing in Siddham script - https://archive.org/details/mahavairocanasutrachikyoyamamotoadityaprakashan_769_B/page/54/mode/1up [[Special:Contributions/~2026-10972-22|~2026-10972-22]] ([[User talk:~2026-10972-22|talk]]) 14:46, 18 February 2026 (UTC)

I myself have only a minute knowledge of Buddhism, therefore, I would like somebody with a better insight to comment. -- [[User:Emana|Emana]] 07:22, 24 February 2007 (UTC) :It appears that the merge has been done, but currently it's not clear what the connection is between Fudo and Acala. Perhaps someone can explain, with sources: Are they the variations of the same god, or merely similar? --[[User:Chriswaterguy|Chriswaterguy]] [[User talk:Chriswaterguy|talk]] 20:55, 23 August 2007 (UTC) ::My understanding is that they're the same thing, and Fudo myo-o (lit. immovable wisdom king) is just a translation of the meaning into Chinese/Japanese, as opposed to the Sanskrit reading. [[User:Jpatokal|Jpatokal]] 06:00, 24 August 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:10, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==LGBT?== According to LGBT themes in mythology, this figure is somehow LGBT-related. Does anyone know how, even if it is disputed? If not, i've tagged it for removal[[User:Yobmod|Yob]][[User talk:Yobmod|Mod]] 13:51, 20 March 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:10, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Korean/Chinese == Same Chinese characters reads 'Bu-dong myung-wang' in Korean. We should add Chinese characters and also Korean reading since it is popular in both areas. --[[Special:Contributions/128.227.69.240|128.227.69.240]] ([[User talk:128.227.69.240|talk]]) 22:28, 5 April 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:10, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Nice Met photos == Some nice Met acalas [http://www.metmuseum.org/art/collection/search#!/search?q=acala&perPage=20&sortBy=Relevance&sortOrder=asc&offset=0&pageSize=0 here]. Will add one or two when they get moved to Commons. —[[User:LuisVilla|Luis]] ([[User talk:LuisVilla|talk]]) 05:07, 11 February 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:10, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Errors in the mantra == According to my link, the mantra is also written in IAST, but based on the traditional writing in Siddham script - https://archive.org/details/mahavairocanasutrachikyoyamamotoadityaprakashan_769_B/page/54/mode/1up [[Special:Contributions/~2026-10972-22|~2026-10972-22]] ([[User talk:~2026-10972-22|talk]]) 14:46, 18 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:10, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:不動明王, en:Acala); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:10, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:39, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:04, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 18:42, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/53510767 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) 「真言を唱えることが越三昧耶戒に当たる」という説、様々な仏教関係書らしきものを典拠に書かれていましたが、不動明王信仰と本来何も関係がない上、ポピュラーな解説書では見たことも聞いたこともない奇説でしたので、削除致しました。あまりにも長文ですので残しようがなく４分の３を削りました。一つここの文章をお書きになった方に伺いたいのですが、不動信仰を行っている現代日本の諸寺院のうちで真言を唱えるなといっている寺院があるようでしたら、是非名前を出して頂ければと思います。私も不動信仰がガキの頃からでずいぶん長い人間ですし所々の不動奉安寺院にも参拝しましたが寡聞にして聞いたことのない説です。 --[[利用者:Touryuuuan|松平俊介]]（[[利用者‐会話:Touryuuuan|会話]]） 2014年11月13日 (木) 14:21 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1339016547 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=B|1= {{WikiProject Buddhism|importance=Mid}} {{WikiProject Japan|myth=|importance=mid|religion=y}} {{WikiProject China|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject East Asia|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Religion|importance=Top}} }} == Untitled == It appears to be odd that Acala is designated as cognate with Vairocana, rather than Akshobya - especially as Akshobya also means "The Immovable One" - however, I am not skilled in Shingon. So this is more of a query. "Akshobya is sometimes merged with Acala (Jp. Fudo myo-o), whose name also means the 'Immovable One' in Sanskrit. However, Acalanatha is not a Buddha, but one of the Five Wisdom Kings of the Womb Realm in Vajrayana. Prior to the advent of Bhaisajyaguru (Yakushi), Akshobya was the subject of a minor popular cult in Japan as healing Buddha." - http://www.sortlifeout.com/HH_Dalai_Lama-Glasgow2004-SECC.htm Appears to agree with me - so I shall make the change. ([[User:20040302|20040302]]) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Proposed merger == It has been proposed that [[Fudo]] be incorporated into [[Acala]]. I found the oneway merge tag and changed it so it can be referenced back. I myself have only a minute knowledge of Buddhism, therefore, I would like somebody with a better insight to comment. -- [[User:Emana|Emana]] 07:22, 24 February 2007 (UTC) :It appears that the merge has been done, but currently it's not clear what the connection is between Fudo and Acala. Perhaps someone can explain, with sources: Are they the variations of the same god, or merely similar? --[[User:Chriswaterguy|Chriswaterguy]] [[User talk:Chriswaterguy|talk]] 20:55, 23 August 2007 (UTC) ::My understanding is that they're the same thing, and Fudo myo-o (lit. immovable wisdom king) is just a translation of the meaning into Chinese/Japanese, as opposed to the Sanskrit reading. [[User:Jpatokal|Jpatokal]] 06:00, 24 August 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==LGBT?== According to LGBT themes in mythology, this figure is somehow LGBT-related. Does anyone know how, even if it is disputed? If not, i've tagged it for removal[[User:Yobmod|Yob]][[User talk:Yobmod|Mod]] 13:51, 20 March 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Korean/Chinese == Same Chinese characters reads 'Bu-dong myung-wang' in Korean. We should add Chinese characters and also Korean reading since it is popular in both areas. --[[Special:Contributions/128.227.69.240|128.227.69.240]] ([[User talk:128.227.69.240|talk]]) 22:28, 5 April 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Nice Met photos == Some nice Met acalas [http://www.metmuseum.org/art/collection/search#!/search?q=acala&perPage=20&sortBy=Relevance&sortOrder=asc&offset=0&pageSize=0 here]. Will add one or two when they get moved to Commons. —[[User:LuisVilla|Luis]] ([[User talk:LuisVilla|talk]]) 05:07, 11 February 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Errors in the mantra == According to my link, the mantra is also written in IAST, but based on the traditional writing in Siddham script - https://archive.org/details/mahavairocanasutrachikyoyamamotoadityaprakashan_769_B/page/54/mode/1up [[Special:Contributions/~2026-10972-22|~2026-10972-22]] ([[User talk:~2026-10972-22|talk]]) 14:46, 18 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

「真言を唱えることが越三昧耶戒に当たる」という説、様々な仏教関係書らしきものを典拠に書かれていましたが、不動明王信仰と本来何も関係がない上、ポピュラーな解説書では見たことも聞いたこともない奇説でしたので、削除致しました。あまりにも長文ですので残しようがなく４分の３を削りました。一つここの文章をお書きになった方に伺いたいのですが、不動信仰を行っている現代日本の諸寺院のうちで真言を唱えるなといっている寺院があるようでしたら、是非名前を出して頂ければと思います。私も不動信仰がガキの頃からでずいぶん長い人間ですし所々の不動奉安寺院にも参拝しましたが寡聞にして聞いたことのない説です。 --[[User:Touryuuuan|松平俊介]]（[[利用者‐会話:Touryuuuan|会話]]） 2014年11月13日 (木) 14:21 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1339016547 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=B|1= {{WikiProject Buddhism|importance=Mid}} {{WikiProject Japan|myth=|importance=mid|religion=y}} {{WikiProject China|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject East Asia|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Religion|importance=Top}} }} == Untitled == It appears to be odd that Acala is designated as cognate with Vairocana, rather than Akshobya - especially as Akshobya also means "The Immovable One" - however, I am not skilled in Shingon. So this is more of a query. "Akshobya is sometimes merged with Acala (Jp. Fudo myo-o), whose name also means the 'Immovable One' in Sanskrit. However, Acalanatha is not a Buddha, but one of the Five Wisdom Kings of the Womb Realm in Vajrayana. Prior to the advent of Bhaisajyaguru (Yakushi), Akshobya was the subject of a minor popular cult in Japan as healing Buddha." - http://www.sortlifeout.com/HH_Dalai_Lama-Glasgow2004-SECC.htm Appears to agree with me - so I shall make the change. ([[User:20040302|20040302]]) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Proposed merger == It has been proposed that [[Fudo]] be incorporated into [[Acala]]. I found the oneway merge tag and changed it so it can be referenced back. I myself have only a minute knowledge of Buddhism, therefore, I would like somebody with a better insight to comment. -- [[User:Emana|Emana]] 07:22, 24 February 2007 (UTC) :It appears that the merge has been done, but currently it's not clear what the connection is between Fudo and Acala. Perhaps someone can explain, with sources: Are they the variations of the same god, or merely similar? --[[User:Chriswaterguy|Chriswaterguy]] [[User talk:Chriswaterguy|talk]] 20:55, 23 August 2007 (UTC) ::My understanding is that they're the same thing, and Fudo myo-o (lit. immovable wisdom king) is just a translation of the meaning into Chinese/Japanese, as opposed to the Sanskrit reading. [[User:Jpatokal|Jpatokal]] 06:00, 24 August 2007 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==LGBT?== According to LGBT themes in mythology, this figure is somehow LGBT-related. Does anyone know how, even if it is disputed? If not, i've tagged it for removal[[User:Yobmod|Yob]][[User talk:Yobmod|Mod]] 13:51, 20 March 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Korean/Chinese == Same Chinese characters reads 'Bu-dong myung-wang' in Korean. We should add Chinese characters and also Korean reading since it is popular in both areas. --[[Special:Contributions/128.227.69.240|128.227.69.240]] ([[User talk:128.227.69.240|talk]]) 22:28, 5 April 2009 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Nice Met photos == Some nice Met acalas [http://www.metmuseum.org/art/collection/search#!/search?q=acala&perPage=20&sortBy=Relevance&sortOrder=asc&offset=0&pageSize=0 here]. Will add one or two when they get moved to Commons. —[[User:LuisVilla|Luis]] ([[User talk:LuisVilla|talk]]) 05:07, 11 February 2017 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Errors in the mantra == According to my link, the mantra is also written in IAST, but based on the traditional writing in Siddham script - https://archive.org/details/mahavairocanasutrachikyoyamamotoadityaprakashan_769_B/page/54/mode/1up [[Special:Contributions/~2026-10972-22|~2026-10972-22]] ([[User talk:~2026-10972-22|talk]]) 14:46, 18 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:27, 25 February 2026 (UTC)
